objective: C3A-CP5 implements the next Checkpoint 3A child checkpoint: an executable access/quota/retention probe that executes the local product/API path and evaluates runtime API-visible access boundaries, deterministic local quota/resource behavior, retention/deletion evidence, and replay behavior. Positive claims: C3A-CP5-HARNESS-001 dispatches the access/quota/retention probe as an implemented executable pytest probe with shell=False and bounded/redacted failure output while preserving CP1 API E2E, CP2 output-correctness, CP3 language-quality, and CP4 media-artifacts probes; C3A-CP5-AQR-001 creates approved synthetic local projects through API behavior, uploads approved synthetic knowledge, ingests/chunks/stores it, generates grounded walkthrough output, exercises project/run/document/artifact access boundaries, rejects cross-project and mismatched source-run replay, verifies idempotency replay cannot bypass access checks, verifies local upload/request/body/document limits fail safely, and verifies existing deletion/tombstone evidence cannot be replayed as active evidence when policy forbids it; C3A-CP5-NEG-001 proves docs/prose/static-snapshot, status-only, and canned-success behavior inadequate. Later security/observability, performance, and real-browser E2E probes remain planned and do not complete Checkpoint 3A. Repository-ledger cleanup for PR #262 and issue #261 may be bundled because this is a substantive child checkpoint rather than a standalone status-only follow-up.
